title: The Passport (Third Amendment) Rules, 1972 (30.08.1972)
text: REGISTERED No. D. 221 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Subsection (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 227] NEW DELHI, WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 30, 1972/BHADRA 8, 1893 Separate paging is given to this Part in order that it may be filed as a separate compilation MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATIONS New Delhi, the 30th August 1972 G.S.R. 397(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 24 of the Passports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the passports Rules, 1967, namely: — 1. (1) These rules may be called the Passports (Third Amendment) Rules, Rules),— (2) They shall come into force on the 1st September, 1972. 2. In rule 10 of the Passports Rules, 1967 (hereinafter referred to as the said rules),— (i) to sub-rule (1), the following proviso shall be added, namely:— "Provided that an India-Bangladesh passport shall continue in force for a period of three years from the date of its issue."; (ii) sub-rule (4) shall he omitted. 3. In Schedule II to the said rules, under the heading "Part II—TRAVEL DOCUMENTS", item No. i n and the entries relating thereto shall be omitted. (1051) -- 1 of 24 -- 1052 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY IPART II— 4. Date Amount issued or refunded (Sterling or rupees equivalent to nearest £ or Re.) Stamp and signature of Autho- rised Dealer selling exchange. (Inside back cover) Hindi Version IMPORTANT 1. This passport is the property of the Government of India, and may, at the discretion of that Government, at any time be revoked, and where the passport is so revoked, it shall be lawful for that Government to retain its possession if it is in its possession and to recover its possession if it is not in its possesion. 2. In case of loss of this passport, that fact and the circumstances leading to the loss must be reported immediately to the issuing authority and the nearest police station or, if loss occurs while in Bangladesh, to the nearest Indian Diplo- matic Mission and the local police. 3. This passport must not be altered or multiiated In any way nor any endorse- ment made on it by any person other than a duly authorised official. Regulations 1. Application for renewal must be made before the date of expiry of the passport and normally not more than three months before such expiry. 2. The possession of this passport does not exempt the bearer from compliance with Visa and Immigration regulations of Bangladesh and emigration rules of India. 3. (i) A separate passport must be taken out for each person above fifteen years of age. (ii) Children under fifteen years of age may be included In the passport of either parent whom they accompany. 4. This passport should not be sent out of the country of residence by post. [No. VI/401/50/72.] -- 7 of 24 -- 1058 THE OA2BTTE OF INDIA BXTftAORDWAftY ffA»T II— -- 8 of 24 -- «BS.
